items.markdown:
Master data management (MDM) is `a comprehensive discipline and technology used to create a single, unified "golden record" or single source of truth for an organization's critical business data` . It integrates, cleanses, and synchronizes data—such as customer, product, and supplier information—across all enterprise systems.[](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/master-data-management) [[1]](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/master-data-management)[[2]](https://www.sap.com/sea/products/data-cloud/master-data-governance/what-is-mdm.html)[[3]](https://www.sap.com/resources/what-is-mdm)

Core Components of MDM

- **Master Data:** The core entities that drive business, including customers (who), products (what), locations (where), and suppliers.
- **Processes:** The automated and manual workflows established to de-duplicate, reconcile, and continuously maintain data quality.
- **Governance:** The people and policies—such as data stewards and quality councils—that manage data ownership and compliance.[](https://www.informatica.com/resources/articles/what-is-master-data-management.html) [[1]](https://www.informatica.com/resources/articles/what-is-master-data-management.html)[[2]](https://www.masterdata.co.za/index.php/mdm-solutions)

Why MDM is Essential

- **Eliminates Silos:** Prevents different departments (like sales, finance, and marketing) from keeping inconsistent, disjointed records of the same customer or product.[](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/master-data-management) [[1]](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/master-data-management)
- **Improves Decision-Making:** Provides trusted, high-quality data for business intelligence (BI) and reliable artificial intelligence (AI) models.[](https://www.sap.com/resources/what-is-mdm) [[1]](https://www.sap.com/resources/what-is-mdm)
- **Enhances Efficiency:** Reduces the time wasted on data cleanup, prevents costly redundancies, and ensures smoother day-to-day operations.[](https://www.informatica.com/resources/articles/what-is-master-data-management.html) [[1]](https://www.informatica.com/resources/articles/what-is-master-data-management.html)[[2]](https://www.sap.com/resources/what-is-mdm)

Leading Platforms & Resources Organizations looking to implement or research MDM often leverage enterprise solutions from major providers. Explore these resources to learn more about industry-standard frameworks and software capabilities:[[1]](https://www.oracle.com/africa/scm/product-lifecycle-management/master-data-management/)[[2]](https://budibase.com/blog/data/data-management-software/)

- [IBM Master Data Management](https://www.ibm.com/products/master-data-management) 
- [Oracle Master Data Management](https://www.oracle.com/africa/scm/product-lifecycle-management/master-data-management/) 
- [SAP Master Data Governance](https://www.sap.com/sea/products/data-cloud/master-data-governance/what-is-mdm.html) 
- [Informatica MDM](https://www.informatica.com/resources/articles/what-is-master-data-management.html)

items.title: What are the 4 types of MDM?

expanded_element.title: What are MDM Implementation Styles? - Reltio
expanded_element.description: Four MDM implementation styles are common, Registry model, Consolidation model, Coexistence model, and Centralized model, with a fifth model, E-Synchronization model, that is the latest evolving model with deeper connections into e-commerce platforms to accommodate increasing consumer preferences for online shopping.

items.title: What does a master data management do?

expanded_element.title: What is Master Data Management (MDM)? - Informatica
expanded_element.description: Master data management (MDM) involves creating a single master record for each person, place, or thing in a business, from across internal and external data sources and applications. This information has been de-duplicated, reconciled and enriched, becoming a consistent, reliable source.

items.title: Is MDM outdated?

expanded_element.title: Master Data Management Isn't Dead—It's Evolving: Here's How
expanded_element.description: The short answer: Master Data Management isn't dead—it's evolving. Rather than fading into obsolescence, MDM is adapting to modern demands. Traditional implementations focused primarily on centralizing static data like customer, product, or supplier records.
